author: Ameen Hassan
summary: The session covered three main topics. First, the assistant explored the Workshop_1_Files directory and identified that 'the vault' referenced in a Welcome.md file was an Obsidian vault — the Workshop/ subdirectory is configured as an Obsidian vault for Building Bridges Workshop 1 materials. Second, the user asked about their session memory project; the assistant retrieved full AgentOS project memory from the ameen-context MCP server and summarized its architecture, status, and open threads. Third, the user asked about their IDeaS projects; the assistant queried the ideas-lab project memory from the MCP server and began retrieving sprint-based work context for the IDeaS Revenue Solutions internship.
problems: Unclear what 'the vault' referred to in the Workshop_1_Files context
User wanted a current status summary of the AgentOS session memory project
User wanted a current status summary of their IDeaS Revenue Solutions work
approaches: Listed Workshop/ subdirectory contents to identify the .obsidian config folder, confirming Obsidian vault setup
Called context_get_project_memory for project 'agentos' via ameen-context MCP
Called context_get_project_memory for project 'ideas-lab' via ameen-context MCP
results: Workshop/ folder confirmed as an Obsidian vault for Building Bridges Workshop 1 materials; Welcome.md is the standard Obsidian onboarding note

AgentOS is a session memory layer on the ameen-context MCP server with SessionStart/SessionEnd hooks, a project classifier, and a backfill pipeline; 19 valid queue entries remain after a 2026-05-25 data cleanup
ideas-lab project memory retrieved successfully, tracking IDeaS.Lab internship in a sprint-based structure
open_questions: What is the full current status and next steps for the IDeaS ideas-lab sprint work?
Are the 19 remaining queue entries in AgentOS valid and ready for the next synthesis pass?
Is end-to-end hook registration stable after the last install_hooks.sh run?
